rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth resolution (regression from #4527, fixed by merged PR #4792, already folded into the vLLM 0.24 rebase commit a560ed18) rather than treating it as a live bug, matching the thread exactly. Y goes further: it traces the actual mechanism end-to-end (scheduler's _FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES → accumulate_full_payload_output gating → connector timeout/FINISHED_ERROR logs → why async_chunk:true never hit this via partition_payload_list), giving much richer, more falsifiable grounding than X's file/line list alone. Y is also more complete for the reporter's specific situation, preemptively addressing likely red herrings in their env (NVFP4 patch line, unrelated min_tokens/empty-audio issue, 300s→600s timeout change) and offering an upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround menu, whereas X's resolution section is comparatively terse and generic.
